selection.status_reason: Live thesis monitor refreshed the active weekly pick.

reasons: Price trend remains constructive: 5-day momentum is 4.8% and 20-day momentum is 12.7%.
Trend quality is supported by 70% positive sessions over the last 10 trading days and the price sitting 7.0% relative to its 20-day average.
Risk profile shows daily volatility around 1.2%, downside volatility near 0.9%, and a recent max drawdown of 2.7%.
Volume confirmation is -34.8% versus the trailing baseline, which helps separate steady breakouts from thin momentum.
Relative strength is 1.8% versus SPY over 5 days and 0.4% versus the sector ETF.
News signal is bullish with score 0.75, confidence 0.63, and 0.1 weighted articles across 3 sources.
GPT-5 article classification and company-news provider coverage rated the news flow bullish at 0.75, after adjusting for relevance, recency, source quality, and story concentration.
Evidence quality: 3 articles across 3 sources, 0.1 effective weighted articles, average relevance 0.58, average source quality 0.75, confidence 0.63.
Technical weights were calibrated on 2303 historical symbol-weeks against 5-day excess return (IC 0.00).
Overall model score is 0.167 with medium confidence.

news_evidence.title: The SpaceX IPO Just Handed Wall Street's Big Banks a Huge Payday. Here's What It Means for Their Stocks.
news_evidence.llm_reason: Covers the SpaceX IPO underwriting windfall for major investment banks. Likely benefits large banks' investment-banking revenue and sentiment (which can lift BAC modestly), but the piece is sector-level and does not provide BAC-specific fee allocations or guaranteed near-term impact.

news_evidence.title: US stocks: Dow hits record high as oil slides; SpaceX shares soar
news_evidence.llm_reason: General market recap (Dow record, oil slide, SpaceX shares). Could influence BAC via broad market sentiment/flows but contains no BAC-specific developments—limited predictive value for BAC over the next 1–10 trading days.
